title: Cheap May Half-Term Flights 2026 — Best Deals from Manchester, Gatwick & Bristol
subtitle: May 23–31 half-term fares are still reasonable from Manchester, Gatwick and Bristol — but not for much longer
airport_names: Gatwick, Stansted, Manchester, Birmingham, Bristol, Edinburgh
meta: Cheap May half-term flights 2026 from Manchester, Gatwick and Bristol. Best destinations, current prices, and how to book before fares spike. Family flight deals May 23-31.

sections.heading: Cheap May half-term flights — why this window is the trickiest to get right
sections.body:
Cheap May half-term flights are still available but the booking window is tightening. May half-term falls roughly May 23–31 for most English and Welsh schools in 2026, overlapping directly with the Spring Bank Holiday on Monday May 25. That combination — school break plus public holiday — makes it the most demand-compressed non-summer period of the year, and finding cheap May half-term flights requires acting well before the half-term week itself.

Unlike Easter, where last-minute prices can sometimes stay manageable on less popular routes, May half-term has a narrower window and a more uniform demand spike. Families with school-age children all want the same dates, and there's no flexibility around the school calendar the way there is for adults-only travel.

The window to book cheap May half-term flights at reasonable prices is closing now. From mid-April, prices will begin repricing sharply. By early May — as the half-term week becomes imminent — anything not already booked will be at premium pricing or sold out.

sections.heading: Best cheap May half-term flight deals from Manchester
sections.body:
Manchester Airport is the strongest departure point for cheap May half-term flights in the North and Midlands. Jet2's extensive network from Manchester — with inclusive bag pricing — consistently delivers the best total value for families, and the route coverage for half-term is comprehensive.

Manchester to Faro (FAO), Algarve — Jet2: Around £70–95 one-way with 22kg bags included for May 23 departures. The Algarve in late May is close to ideal: 22–24°C, uncrowded compared to July, and accommodation significantly cheaper than summer. Best overall family value from Manchester for half-term.

Manchester to Menorca (MAH) — Jet2: Around £70–90 one-way with bags for May 23. Menorca from Manchester is consistently underpriced relative to Mallorca from the same airport — and the island experience is arguably better: quieter, cleaner beaches, no stag-do atmosphere.

Manchester to Malaga (AGP) — Jet2 or Ryanair: Around £80–110 one-way for May 22–23. Costa del Sol in late May is fully open — 22–24°C, sea swimmable, and the tourist infrastructure running well without peak-season crowds.

Manchester to Marrakech (RAK) — easyJet: Around £65–85 one-way for May 23. Marrakech at 26–32°C in late May is different and rewarding for older children or adults — the souks, Jardin Majorelle, day trips to the Atlas Mountains.

sections.heading: Best cheap May half-term flights from Gatwick and Bristol
sections.body:
Gatwick provides the widest range of routes for May half-term from a London airport, with easyJet's hub there offering strong competition on Mediterranean and Atlantic destinations.

Gatwick to Menorca (MAH) — easyJet: Around £65–85 one-way for May 23. Menorca doesn't reprice as aggressively as Mallorca because it's less famous — and that makes it the most consistent value pick for a Gatwick departure at half-term.

Gatwick to Faro (FAO), Algarve — easyJet: Around £60–85 one-way for May 23. The Algarve from Gatwick is a two-hour flight to reliable warm weather and beaches. One of the best half-term options from a London airport.

Gatwick to Marrakech (RAK) — easyJet: Around £65–85 one-way for May 23. Three and a half hours from Gatwick to a completely different experience. Marrakech works particularly well for couples or families with teenagers.

Bristol (BRS) to Faro (FAO) — Ryanair: Around £55–75 one-way for May 22–23. Bristol to the Algarve is a two-hour flight and one of the shortest routes to proper beach weather from any UK airport. Exceptionally good value for South West families.

Bristol to Malaga (AGP) — Ryanair: Around £60–85 one-way for May 23. Bristol–Malaga is direct and fast — Bristol Airport is significantly quicker to navigate at bank holiday time than Gatwick or Manchester.

sections.heading: May half-term routes that are already repriced
sections.body:
Some destinations have already priced in the half-term premium. Palma, Mallorca (PMI) from Gatwick or Manchester is the clearest example: Jet2 from Manchester is already showing £120–150 one-way for May 23, which is the same pricing it holds in peak July. The route is simply too popular to find value this close to half-term.

Alicante (ALC) from Manchester on easyJet is similarly gone — £105–135 one-way for May 23. Not outrageously expensive, but not the deal it should be for early booking. Barcelona (BCN) is even worse: £130–175 one-way from Gatwick for the bank holiday weekend.

If you haven't booked these routes already, the better strategy is to look at their less-famous alternatives: Menorca instead of Mallorca. Faro instead of Alicante. Marrakech instead of Barcelona. The experience quality is comparable or better; the prices are consistently lower because demand doesn't concentrate the same way.

sections.heading: Practical tips for booking May half-term flights
sections.body:
Compare Jet2 total prices carefully. Jet2 includes 22kg checked luggage per person, a 10kg carry-on, and seat selection for families. For a family of four, that's potentially £120–160 in bag fees saved versus Ryanair or easyJet. On routes Jet2 serves — primarily from Manchester, Leeds, Birmingham — they're often the best total value.

Saturday May 23 vs Friday May 22. Prices on Friday May 22 (the last school day for many) are lower than Saturday May 23. If your children finish school at noon on Friday and you can get to the airport, the Friday departure is meaningfully cheaper on most routes — £25–50 per person less.

Check accommodation before locking in flights. May half-term accommodation in the Algarve, Menorca and Mallorca is in high demand. The worst outcome is finding cheap flights and then discovering that all suitable accommodation is either gone or wildly priced. Confirm a hotel or villa first, then book flights around it.

Return on Sunday May 31 or Monday June 1, not Saturday May 30. The Saturday return is peak pricing — everyone flying Saturday out wants to return the following Saturday. Sunday or Monday returns are consistently £30–60 per person cheaper on popular sun routes.

sections.heading: Why Manchester beats London airports for May half-term families
sections.body:
The case for flying from Manchester at May half-term is stronger than most people realise. Jet2's headquarters at Manchester means the most comprehensive inclusive-pricing network for family travellers — bags included, seat selection included, no death-by-a-thousand-extras that turns a £69 Ryanair fare into a £140 reality.

For a family of four with 22kg checked bags each, the Jet2 total from Manchester to Faro at £85 one-way per person (£340 total) often beats Ryanair from Gatwick at £65 per person headline + £35 per bag × 4 bags = £400 total. Manchester wins the real cost comparison frequently.

Manchester Airport itself is also easier to navigate at half-term than Gatwick or Stansted. The queue times for security and check-in at Manchester are lower than London airports during peak school holiday departures, and the terminal layout is more manageable with children.

From Birmingham, the same logic applies for Midlands families — Jet2 from Birmingham to Faro, Malaga or the Canaries at inclusive prices consistently beats the London airport alternative when you factor in transport costs to Gatwick or Stansted.
